title: Edges and extraction: Evidence from Chichewa
author: Kenyon Branan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
A growing body of work argues that Agree has the effect of “unlocking” certain 
domains (phases) such that otherwise illicit extraction from them becomes permitted. 
However, existing proposals disagree on whether Agree is in fact always required 
to Unlock phases for extraction, or only required for extractions that would 
otherwise bypass the phase edge. We argue that constraints on extraction from DP in 
Chichewa reported in Mchombo (2004, 2006) provide evidence for the latter 
theory, which privileges phase edges. We go on to show that this theory makes correct 
predictions about Dinka, the language Van Urk & Richards (2015) originally argued 
provides evidence for the opposite conclusion.
steps_raw:
A growing body of work argues that Agree has the effect of “unlocking” certain 
domains (phases) such that otherwise illicit extraction from them becomes permitted. 
However, existing proposals disagree on whether Agree is in fact always required 
to Unlock phases for extraction, or only required for extractions that would 
otherwise bypass the phase edge. We argue that constraints on extraction from DP in 
Chichewa reported in Mchombo (2004, 2006) provide evidence for the latter 
theory, which privileges phase edges. We go on to show that this theory makes correct 
predictions about Dinka, the language Van Urk & Richards (2015) originally argued 
provides evidence for the opposite conclusion.
